hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium (58 mg/100 g) and high potassium (285 mg/100 g) yield a favourable sodium-to-potassium ratio aligned with DASH dietary principles.
hypertension.guidance: Raw frog leg is suitable for hypertensive individuals in its uncooked state; however, final sodium content is highly dependent on cooking method — avoid heavy soy sauce, oyster sauce, or salted black bean preparations, and note that claypot frog leg porridge can deliver substantially elevated sodium from added seasonings.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero carbohydrates and zero sugar with high protein (16.4 g/100 g) produce no glycaemic load, supporting stable postprandial blood glucose.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Raw frog leg is an excellent protein source for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to its zero carbohydrate content and negligible fat; cooking method must be controlled — avoid sweet sauces, starchy thickeners, or rice-based claypot preparations that add significant carbohydrate load.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Very low total fat (0.3 g/100 g), low saturated fat (0.1 g/100 g), moderate cholesterol (50 mg/100 g), and high potassium support a heart-healthy nutritional profile.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Raw frog leg is a lean, low-saturated-fat protein source comparable to skinless chicken breast and is appropriate for cardiovascular disease management; individuals should avoid deep-frying or high-sodium sauce preparations that negate its favourable lipid and electrolyte profile.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Lean white meat with negligible saturated fat and no omega-3 fatty acids; absence of positive anti-inflammatory factors means neutrality rather than benefit for RA.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Raw frog leg does not worsen rheumatoid arthritis due to its lean profile, but it provides no active anti-inflammatory benefit (no omega-3, antioxidants, or polyphenols); pair with omega-3-rich foods and avoid deep-frying or high-sodium preparations that may exacerbate RA-related tissue swelling.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Raw frog leg (田鸡腿, Kaki katak mentah) is the uncooked hind leg of the frog, sold bone-in with pale pink, tender white meat. It is a popular ingredient in Singaporean and Southeast Asian cuisine, commonly prepared in claypot dishes, stir-fries with ginger and spring onion, or porridge. The texture is delicate and mild, often compared to chicken breast or fish. Nutritionally, raw frog leg is a high-protein (16.4 g/100 g), very low-fat (0.3 g/100 g), zero-carbohydrate food delivering only 73 kcal per 100 g, making it one of the leanest animal proteins available. Sodium is low at 58 mg/100 g and potassium is high at 285 mg/100 g, providing an excellent electrolyte ratio. Cholesterol is moderate at 50 mg/100 g. It is suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, and cardiovascular disease in its raw or simply prepared form. Cooking method is critical — heavy sauces, deep-frying, or rice-based preparations significantly alter its nutritional profile. Frog leg contains no gluten and no dairy, and is free of common allergens in its raw state.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F06080005) at confidence 0.95, which is a primary approved regional source for Singapore foods. The record is marked as reviewed and canonical provenance.
notes: Carbohydrate_g is confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo database — raw frog leg is an animal protein containing no carbohydrates (naturally zero). Sugar_g is naturally zero as this food contains no carbohydrates. Dietary_fibre_g is naturally zero as this is an animal protein product with no plant-derived fibre. Saturated_fat_g of 0.1 g/100 g is sourced from USDA FoodData Central (NDB 17169, Frog legs, raw) as a supplementary reference consistent with the HPB total fat value of 0.3 g/100 g. Monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, full vitamin panel, and moisture data are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo entry for this food item. USDA FoodData Central (NDB 17169) provides partial data for some micronutrients but does not fully align with the HPB Singapore entry; these fields are left as -1 pending a dedicated cross-source reconciliation. Raw frog leg; glycemic index is not applicable as this food contains negligible carbohydrates (0.0 g/100 g). The glycemic_index field is set to the numeric sentinel value 0 (template default) with gi_category set to not_applicable to satisfy the validator requirement for a numeric type while correctly communicating that no GI value is scientifically assignable for this negligible-carbohydrate animal protein. Halal status is not confirmed in the HPB source — frog is considered non-halal under Shafi'i school of Islamic jurisprudence followed in Singapore; is_halal is set to false. FoodOn URI and FoodEx2 code are defensible best-match assignments; SNOMED CT coverage for raw frog leg as a food product is absent.
